content.problemStatement:
Harry is a bright student. To prepare thoroughly for exams, he completes all the exercises in his book! Now that the exams are approaching fast, he is doing book exercises day and night. He writes down and keeps updating the remaining number of exercises on the back cover of each book.

Harry has a lot of books messed on the floor. Therefore, he wants to pile up the books **that still have some remaining exercises** into a single pile. He will grab the books one-by-one and add the books that still have remaining exercises to the top of the pile.

Whenever he wants to do a book exercise, he will pick the book with the minimum number of remaining exercises from the pile. In order to pick the book, he has to remove all the books above it. Therefore, if there are more than one books with the minimum number of remaining exercises, he will take the one which requires the least number of books to remove. The removed books are returned to the messy floor. After he picks the book, he will do all the remaining exercises and trash the book.

Since number of books is rather large, he needs your help to tell him the number of books he must remove, for picking the book with the minimum number of exercises.


Note that more than one book can have the same name.
constraints: 0 <= (the number of remaining exercises of each book) < 100000
The name of each book consists of between 1 and 15 characters 'a' - 'z'.
Whenever he wants to do a book exercise, there is at least one book in the pile.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer N denoting the number of actions. Then N lines follow. Each line starts with an integer. If the integer is -1, that means Harry wants to do a book exercise. Otherwise, the integer is the number of the remaining exercises in the book he grabs next. This is followed by a string denoting the name of the book.
content.outputFormat: For each -1 in the input, output a single line containing the number of books Harry must remove, followed by the name of the book that Harry must pick.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first -1, the book with the minimum number of exercises is mathematics. Harry has to remove 1 book from the top to pick mathematics and solve the remaining exercises. For the second -1, the book on the top has the least number of remaining exercises. Thus, Harry has to remove 0 books from the top to pick up graphics book.

For the first -1, the book with the minimum number of exercises is biology. Harry has to remove 2 books from the top to pick biology and solve the remaining exercises. For the second -1, the book with the minimum number of exercises is chemistry. Harry has to remove 1 book from the top to pick chemistry and solve the remaining exercises.

For the first -1, the book with the minimum number of exercises is english. Harry has to remove 1 book from the top to pick english and solve the remaining exercises. For the second -1, the book on the top has the least number of remaining exercises. Thus, Harry has to remove 0 books from the top to pick up mathematics book.

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.input:
10
10 book1
5 book2
3 book3
-1
-1
10 book4
5 book5
3 book6
-1
-1

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we use a priority queue to store the books. The priority queue is ordered by the number of exercises in the book and then by the position of the book in the pile. When Harry wants to do a book exercise, we remove the book with the minimum number of exercises from the pile and print the number of books that need to be removed to reach this book. We then update the positions of the remaining books in the pile.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case, where N is the number of actions.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the space complexity is dominated by the priority queue.
